On Llama-3-8B-Instruct trained on UltraFeedback under a SimPO similar setup, AdaDPO consistently outperforms DPO on AlpacaEval 2: it achieves higher length-controlled win rates (LC) in 81% of hyperparameter combinations, attains the global best LC (48.3%) and raw win rate (46.1%), and enlarges the LC-over-WR margin in 88% of combinations, indicating effective mitigation of length bias. Additional analyses on KL divergence, reward margin, and reward accuracy confirm that AdaDPO rectifies the gradient imbalance and yields more efficient optimization. Because it operates purely at the loss level, AdaDPO can be dropped into existing preference-based alignment pipelines without changing data collection or model architectures. The method requires only a few lines of code, and the same self-adaptive principle generalizes to a broad family of pairwise contrastive preference losses including SimPO, R-DPO, IPO, CPO, and ORPO.
2605.28440.COMMENT: Proposes AdaDPO, a self-adaptive DPO variant that balances gradient updates between preferred and dispreferred responses, directly improving LLM alignment via a novel loss-level modification.
2605.28440.title_cn: AdaDPO：具有平衡梯度更新的自适应直接偏好优化
2605.28440.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.28440v1宣布类型：新摘要：DPO已成为广泛采用的HLHF替代方案，用于将LLM与人类偏好保持一致，从而消除了对单独奖励模型或RL循环的需要。最近的理论分析揭示了DPO中的不对称梯度行为：损失抑制分散响应的速度远快于促进首选响应，导致模型学会避免糟糕的答案，而不是生成好的答案。我们提出AdaDPO，这是DPO算法的自适应变体，它引入了直接从政策模型的生成概率中推导出的按偏好对、基于停止梯度的系数，并将参考模型的概率作为可选组件。AdaDPO的构建是为了强制优先概率和不优先概率之间的梯度幅度相等;实际实现平衡每个令牌的梯度并应用数字剪裁界限以实现稳定性，同时保留DPO的原始超参数结构。在SimPO类似设置下在UltraFeedback上训练的Llama-3- 8B-Direct上，AdaDPO始终优于AlpacaEval 2上的DPO：它在81%的超参数组合中实现了更高的长度控制获胜率（LC），获得了全球最好的LC（48.3%）和原始获胜率（46.1%），并在88%的组合中扩大了LC与WR的差距，表明有效缓解了长度偏差。对KL分歧、奖励利润和奖励准确性的额外分析证实，AdaDPO纠正了梯度失衡并产生了更有效的优化。由于AdaDPO纯粹在损失水平上运行，因此可以将其放入现有的基于偏好的对齐管道中，而无需更改数据收集或模型架构。该方法只需要几行代码，并且相同的自适应原则可推广到广泛的成对对比偏好损失家族，包括SimPO、R-DPO、IPO、CPO和ORPO。

2605.28213.title: Learning When to Optimize: Verified Optimization Skills from Expert GPU-Kernel Lineages
2605.28213.abstract: arXiv:2605.28213v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: LLM-based agents are increasingly used to generate GPU kernels, but they often know what optimizations to try without knowing when those optimizations are sound. We introduce KLineage, which learns this missing "when" knowledge from expert kernels: instead of relying on forward rollouts, KLineage walks expert implementations backward through validation-gated simplifications and reverses each accepted step into a reusable optimization skill. Each skill records not only the optimization intent, but also where it applies in code, what conditions made it valid, what effect it had, and what failures its assumptions avoid. A downstream LLM materializes these skills on new code surfaces under the same compile/correctness/profile gate. On five expert workloads across two NVIDIA architectures, these lineage-derived skills serve as an effective optimization curriculum, exceeding recent memory-based LLM-kernel baselines in both final kernel quality and optimization efficiency under the same fixed budget. We additionally use a separate 22-instance held-out check as a sanity test against source-case memorization.
2605.28213.COMMENT: Proposes a self-improving agent framework that learns optimization skills from expert GPU-kernel lineages via backward validation, enabling closed-loop capability gain.
2605.28213.title_cn: 学习何时优化：来自专家GPU-Kernell Linages的验证优化技能
2605.28213.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.28213v1宣布类型：新摘要：基于LLM的代理越来越多地用于生成图形处理器内核，但它们通常知道要尝试哪些优化，而不知道这些优化何时有效。我们引入了KLineage，它从专家内核中学习缺失的“何时”知识：KLineage不是依赖于向前展开，而是通过验证门控简化来向后引导专家实现，并将每个接受的步骤逆转为可重复使用的优化技能。每个技能不仅记录优化意图，还记录其在代码中的应用位置、哪些条件使其有效、具有哪些效果以及其假设避免的失败。下游LLM在同一个编译/正确性/配置文件门下的新代码表面上实现这些技能。在跨两种NVIDIA架构的五种专家工作负载上，这些源自谱系的技能可以作为有效的优化课程，在相同的固定预算下，在最终内核质量和优化效率方面都超过了最近基于内存的LLM内核基线。我们还使用一个单独的22个实例hold-out检查作为针对源案例记忆的健全性测试。

2605.28008.title: Zipping the Thought: When and How Compressed Reasoning Data Works in LLM Post-Training
2605.28008.abstract: arXiv:2605.28008v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) can now solve complex problems through long chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning, but the trade-off between performance and token cost remains a central challenge. To address this issue, supervised fine-tuning (SFT) often uses compressed reasoning data, where CoT traces are shortened into compact forms. However, the effect of such compressed reasoning data on post-training remains poorly understood. In this paper, we propose a taxonomy of CoT consisting of Explicit CoT, which outputs all operations without aggregation, Composed CoT, which combines multiple operations into a single step, and Implicit CoT, which omits intermediate operations. We construct a synthetic compositional reasoning task that allows controlled variation of difficulty, compression granularity, and data size, and conducted a comprehensive set of experiments across different model families and sizes. Notably, we find that (i) coarser CoT requires more SFT data, (ii) compared with Explicit CoT, Composed CoT and Implicit CoT benefit more from data scaling, while Composed CoT benefits from data repetition and Implicit CoT tends to lead to memorization, (iii) unlike SFT, subsequent reinforcement learning (RL) with verifiable rewards (RLVR) decomposes compressed steps learned during SFT, and (iv) unidirectional CoT ordering shows stronger generalization on longer sequential tasks. Our findings provide implications for CoT design under data resource constraints and offer important insights into the mechanisms of SFT and RL in LLM post-training.
2605.28008.COMMENT: Provides a systematic study of compressed reasoning data (including implicit CoT) in LLM post-training, with insights on how RL with verifiable rewards decomposes compressed steps, directly relevant to latent reasoning and RL for LLMs.
2605.28008.title_cn: 压缩思想：压缩推理数据何时以及如何在LLM后培训中发挥作用
2605.28008.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.28008v1宣布类型：新摘要：大型语言模型（LLM）现在可以通过长思想链（CoT）推理来解决复杂问题，但性能和代币成本之间的权衡仍然是一个核心挑战。为了解决这个问题，监督式微调（SFT）通常使用压缩推理数据，其中CoT轨迹被缩短为紧凑形式。然而，这种压缩推理数据对训练后的影响仍然知之甚少。本文中，我们提出了一种CoT分类法，由显式CoT（输出所有操作而无需聚合）、组合CoT（将多个操作组合到一个步骤中）和隐式CoT（省略中间操作）组成。我们构建了一个合成合成推理任务，允许控制难度、压缩粒度和数据大小的变化，并在不同的模型系列和大小之间进行了一套全面的实验。值得注意的是，我们发现（i）较粗的CoT需要更多的SFT数据，（ii）与显式CoT相比，合成CoT和隐式CoT从数据缩放中受益更多，而合成CoT从数据重复中受益，而隐式CoT往往会导致记忆，（iii）与SFT不同，具有可验证奖励的后续强化学习（RL）（WLVR）分解SFT期间学习的压缩步骤，（iv）单向CoT排序对较长的顺序任务表现出更强的概括性。我们的研究结果为数据资源限制下的CoT设计提供了启示，并为LLM后培训中SFT和RL的机制提供了重要见解。

2605.27709.title: ReverseMath: Answer Inversion for Scalable and Verifiable Mathematical Problem Generation
2605.27709.abstract: arXiv:2605.27709v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Mathematical reasoning benchmarks are vital for evaluating large language models (LLMs), but many are static and repeatedly exposed through public evaluation and training pipelines, making it difficult to separate genuine reasoning from memorization. Meanwhile, manually constructing new math problems with reliable answers remains costly. We introduce ReverseMath, a scalable method for generating new math problems through answer inversion. Given a problem and its answer, ReverseMath masks a numerical value in the original problem, treats the original answer as a known condition, and rewrites the problem so that the masked value becomes the new answer. The generated problem reverses the original input-output relation, making its answer known by construction. We study ReverseMath for both evaluation and training. For evaluation, paired original/reversed problems reveal substantial behavioral shifts: models sometimes fail on reversed problems and even incorrectly output the original answer, suggesting memorization-like behavior. For training, ReverseMath provides automatically labeled reversed problems as data augmentation for reinforcement learning (RL). Experiments show that including ReverseMath-generated data improves mathematical reasoning performance across multiple benchmarks, demonstrating its value as both an analysis tool and a scalable source of verifiable training data.
2605.27709.COMMENT: Proposes a scalable method for generating verifiable math problems via answer inversion, directly used as data augmentation for RL training of LLMs, strongly matching RL for LLMs criteria.
2605.27709.title_cn: ReverseMath：可扩展和可验证的数学问题生成的答案倒置
2605.27709.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.27709v1宣布类型：新摘要：数学推理基准对于评估大型语言模型（LLM）至关重要，但许多基准是静态的，并且通过公共评估和训练管道反复暴露，因此很难将真正的推理与记忆区分开来。与此同时，手动构建具有可靠答案的新数学问题仍然成本高昂。我们引入ReverseMath，这是一种可扩展的方法，用于通过答案倒置生成新的数学问题。给定一个问题及其答案，ReverseMath隐藏原始问题中的数字值，将原始答案视为已知条件，并重写问题，以便隐藏的值成为新答案。生成的问题颠倒了原始的投入-产出关系，通过构造使其答案已知。我们研究ReverseMath以进行评估和培训。对于评估来说，成对的原始/反向问题揭示了实质性的行为转变：模型有时在反向问题上失败，甚至错误地输出原始答案，这表明类似记忆的行为。对于训练，ReverseMath提供自动标记的反向问题作为强化学习（RL）的数据增强。实验表明，包括ReverseMathh生成的数据可以提高多个基准测试中的数学推理性能，证明了其作为分析工具和可扩展的可验证训练数据源的价值。

2605.27960.title: Mags-RL: Wearing Multimodal LLMs a Magnifying Glass via Agentic Reinforcement Learning For Complex Scene Reasoning
2605.27960.abstract: arXiv:2605.27960v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Despite their popularity and success, Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) often struggle to interpret images accurately, which limits their reasoning capability in complex scenarios (e.g., high object density and complex background clutter). Prior work mainly addresses this limitation by incorporating explicit visual cues like bounding boxes that require extra annotations. In addition, the resulting low-resolution crops often miss fine-grained details that MLLMs require for accurate reasoning. Therefore, we propose Mags-RL, an Agentic Reinforcement Learning (RL) framework that equips MLLMs with an external super-resolution "magnifying glass" agent for high-resolution fine-grained inspection. Specifically, the model performs two-round reasoning: in the first round, it generates an initial rationale and autonomously identifies regions of interest without relying on additional annotations; in the second round, it invokes a super-resolution agent to crop and upscale those regions, then revisits and verifies its earlier reasoning to produce the final answer. We also introduce a novel curriculum learning strategy that enables data-efficient RL training, needing as few as only 40 training samples to achieve reasonable performance. Experiments on VSR, TallyQA, and GQA subsets show its superior performance against recent strong competing methods, demonstrating high-quality reasoning with precise visual grounding. Code and weights will be released soon.
2605.27960.COMMENT: Proposes an agentic RL framework (Mags-RL) that equips MLLMs with a super-resolution agent for fine-grained visual reasoning, using a two-round reasoning loop and curriculum learning.
2605.27960.title_cn: Mags-RL：通过显式强化学习将多模式LLC戴上放大镜进行复杂场景推理
2605.27960.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.27960v1宣布类型：新摘要：尽管多模式大型语言模型（MLLM）很受欢迎并取得了成功，但它们常常难以准确地解释图像，这限制了它们在复杂场景中的推理能力（例如，高物体密度和复杂的背景混乱）。之前的工作主要通过结合明确的视觉线索（例如需要额外注释的边界框）来解决这一局限性。此外，由此产生的低分辨率农作物经常错过MLLM准确推理所需的细粒度细节。因此，我们提出了Mags-RL，这是一种统计强化学习（RL）框架，为MLLM配备了外部超分辨率“放大镜”代理，用于高分辨率细粒度检查。具体来说，该模型执行两轮推理：在第一轮中，它生成初始理由并在不依赖额外注释的情况下自主识别感兴趣的区域;在第二轮中，它调用超分辨率代理来裁剪和放大这些区域，然后重新访问和验证其早期推理以产生最终答案。我们还引入了一种新的课程学习策略，可以实现数据高效的RL训练，只需40个训练样本即可实现合理的性能。在VSR、TallyQA和GQA子集上的实验表明，该方法与最近的强有力的竞争方法相比具有优异的性能，证明了具有精确视觉基础的高质量推理。代码和权重将很快发布。

2605.27483.title: Debate Helps Weak Judges Reward Stronger Models
2605.27483.abstract: arXiv:2605.27483v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Despite theoretical promise, debate as a scalable oversight protocol has produced mixed empirical results: gains in some settings, and null effects in others, especially when the judge does not have information hidden from it. We study proposer-critic debate in a stronger-debater/weaker-judge setting on programmatically verifiable code and logic tasks. Debate helps the judge over a consultancy baseline when the critic provides a usable advantage: the critic's classification ability must exceed the judge's, and the judge must treat critic speeches as claims to verify rather than testimony to summarize.   On the three of five pairings where the condition holds, proposer-critic debate's gains are statistically significant over consultancy, and these pairings are the most capable model pairings. On the two non-responder pairings in our set, debate produces null effects, and judge verification rates drop by tens of percentage points once a critic enters the transcript. In these cases the critic's binary-classification ability and the judge's are within noise of each other, and the critic's disagreement is parsed as testimony rather than a claim to check.   Ablating rebuttal rounds from debate produces no measurable change in judge performance: a single independent critique recovers the bulk of debate's benefit at lower inference cost. These findings suggest a cheaper primitive for training-free scalable oversight in verifiable domains (answer, critique, judge) and a pre-deployment audit (does the critic beat the judge, and will the judge verify it?) that predicts when debate will help.
2605.27483.COMMENT: Proposes debate as a scalable oversight protocol for RL, showing how weak judges can reward stronger models in verifiable domains, directly relevant to RL for LLMs via reward design.
2605.27483.title_cn: 辩论有助于弱法官奖励强模型
2605.27483.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.27483v1宣布类型：新摘要：尽管在理论上有希望，但作为可扩展监督协议的辩论产生了好坏参半的实证结果：在某些情况下是收益，而在其他情况下是无效的，特别是当法官没有隐藏信息时。我们在更强的辩论者/更弱的法官环境中研究了倡导者与批评者的辩论，以可编程验证的代码和逻辑任务。当批评者提供可用优势时，辩论有助于法官确定咨询基线：批评者的分类能力必须超过法官的，法官必须将批评者的演讲视为需要验证的主张，而不是需要总结的证词。   在条件成立的五对中的三对中，宣扬者与批评者的争论在统计上比咨询公司的收益显着，而且这些配对是最有能力的模型配对。在我们集中的两个无响应者配对中，辩论会产生零效应，一旦批评者进入笔录，判断验证率就会下降数十个百分点。在这些情况下，批评者的二进制分类能力和法官的二进制分类能力是相互干扰的，批评者的分歧被解析为证词而不是检查的主张。   从辩论中扣除反驳回合不会对法官的表现产生可衡量的变化：单一的独立批评可以以较低的推理成本恢复辩论的大部分好处。这些发现表明，可以在可验证领域（回答、批评、判断）和部署前审计（批评者是否击败了法官，法官会验证它吗？）中进行免培训的可扩展监督，可以更便宜的原始内容。它预测辩论何时会有所帮助。

2605.27865.title: MERIT: Matching Expertise via Rubric-Informed Training for Reviewer Assignment
2605.27865.abstract: arXiv:2605.27865v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Matching submissions with suitable reviewers at scale is a growing challenge for major venues, yet existing approaches either rely on coarse proxy signals that conflate general relatedness with true suitability, or require expensive human annotations that are difficult to scale for training. We propose MERIT, a two-stage framework that bridges this gap by converting criterion-level expertise matching into scalable suitability supervision. In the first stage, we train a reviewer assessor via reinforcement learning to identify the expertise dimensions a paper requires, match them against the reviewer's prior work, and produce a suitability decision, with rewards provided by an LLM judge guided by paper-specific expertise rubrics. In the second stage, we distill the assessor's predictions into an embedding-based retriever for efficient large-scale assignment. Experiments show that our 4B reviewer assessor outperforms larger general-purpose LLMs on suitability classification, and the resulting retriever achieves state-of-the-art performance across LR-Bench and the CMU Gold dataset. Our code is available at https://github.com/Luli3220/MERIT.
2605.27865.COMMENT: Proposes a two-stage framework using RL with an LLM judge for reviewer assignment, directly matching the RL for LLMs criterion with a novel reward design and training recipe.
2605.27865.title_cn: MerIT：通过审核员任务的知识渊博培训匹配专业知识
2605.27865.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.27865v1宣布类型：新摘要：对于主要场所来说，将提交内容与合适的审阅者进行大规模匹配是一个越来越大的挑战，但现有的方法要么依赖于粗糙的代理信号，将一般相关性与真正的适合性混为一谈，要么需要昂贵的人工注释，难以扩展以进行训练。我们提出了merIT，这是一个两阶段框架，通过将标准级专业知识匹配转化为可扩展的适用性监督来弥合这一差距。在第一阶段，我们通过强化学习培训评审员，以确定论文所需的专业知识维度，将其与评审员之前的工作进行匹配，并做出适合性决定，并由LLM评委在论文特定专业知识指标的指导下提供奖励。在第二阶段，我们将评估者的预测提取到基于嵌入的检索器中，以进行高效的大规模分配。实验表明，我们的4 B审查者评估器在适合性分类方面优于大型通用LLM，并且生成的检索器在LR-Bench和CMU Gold数据集中实现了最先进的性能。我们的代码可在https://github.com/Luli3220/MERIT上获取。

2605.28028.title: BPPO: Binary Prefix Policy Optimization for Efficient GRPO-Style Reasoning RL with Concise Responses
2605.28028.abstract: arXiv:2605.28028v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) is widely used for training reasoning models, but updating all sampled completions in each group incurs substantial cost and can reinforce verbose reasoning trajectories. In this paper, we study whether all completions provide equally useful update signals in GRPO-style reasoning RL. Our gradient-similarity analysis shows that, within the same prompt group, same-class completions often induce highly similar update directions, whereas correct-incorrect pairs provide more distinct contrastive signals. Motivated by this observation, we propose Binary Prefix Policy Optimization (BPPO), which uses the shortest correct completion and the shortest incorrect completion as a compact update unit while preserving full-group advantage normalization. BPPO further improves efficiency with adaptive completion scheduling and prefix-focused optimization; by updating only response prefixes, it avoids reinforcing redundant suffixes and encourages more concise responses. Experiments on GSM8K, MATH, and Geo3K show that BPPO achieves up to 6.08x speedup over GRPO while maintaining competitive accuracy, and reduces mean response length by approximately 30-50% without modifying the reward with an explicit length penalty.
2605.28028.COMMENT: Proposes BPPO, a novel RL method for LLM reasoning that uses binary prefix optimization to improve GRPO efficiency and response conciseness.
2605.28028.title_cn: BPPO：具有简洁响应的高效GRPO式推理RL的二进制后缀策略优化
2605.28028.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.28028v1宣布类型：新摘要：组相对策略优化（GRPO）广泛用于训练推理模型，但更新每个组中的所有采样完成会产生巨大的成本，并且可能会强化冗长的推理轨迹。本文中，我们研究所有完成是否都在GRPO风格推理RL中提供同样有用的更新信号。我们的梯度相似性分析表明，在同一提示组内，同类完成通常会引发高度相似的更新方向，而正确-错误对则会提供更不同的对比信号。受这一观察的启发，我们提出了二进制后缀策略优化（BPPO），它使用最短正确完成和最短错误完成作为紧凑的更新单元，同时保持全组优势规范化。BPPO通过自适应完成调度和以后缀为中心的优化进一步提高了效率;通过仅更新响应后缀，它避免了强化冗余后缀并鼓励更简洁的响应。在GSM 8 K、MATH和Geo 3 K上的实验表明，BPPO在保持有竞争力的准确性的同时，实现了高达6.08倍的加速，并将平均响应长度缩短约30-50%，而无需修改具有显式长度惩罚的奖励。

2605.28490.title: SSR3D-LLM: Structured Spatial Reasoning via Latent Steps for Fine-Grained Grounding in Unified 3D-LLMs
2605.28490.abstract: arXiv:2605.28490v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: 3D object grounding localizes referred objects in a 3D scene from natural language. Unified instance-centric 3D-LLMs aim to solve grounding together with dialog, QA, and captioning, yet many rely on a single pointer-style grounding decision that compresses a relational instruction into one selection. This is brittle for fine-grained queries where multiple same-class candidates must be ruled out by context objects and spatial relations. We propose Structured Spatial Reasoning 3D-LLM (SSR3D-LLM), a structured grounding interface for unified 3D-LLMs. Given fixed Mask3D object proposals, the LLM writes a sequence of latent spatial reasoning steps and memory tokens from the query, and a geometry-aware scorer reads these latent steps in order to refine candidate rankings step by step with step-length masking. The latent steps are learned from standard benchmark target supervision with auxiliary referential-cue supervision during training, while inference uses only the input query and Mask3D proposals. Across ReferIt3D, ScanRefer, and Multi3DRef, SSR3D-LLM achieves the strongest results among unified 3D-LLM baselines, with substantial gains over the single-pointer QPG baseline on fine-grained grounding and consistent improvements over prior unified 3D-LLMs, while preserving the default language-task route.
2605.28490.COMMENT: Introduces latent spatial reasoning steps for 3D grounding, a strong match for latent reasoning architectures that move reasoning into compact latent computation.
2605.28490.title_cn: SSR 3D-LLM：通过潜在步骤进行结构化空间推理，以实现统一3D-LLM中的细粒度基础
2605.28490.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.28490v1宣布类型：新摘要：3D对象基础从自然语言本地化3D场景中引用的对象。统一的以实例为中心的3D-LLM旨在与对话、QA和字幕一起解决基础问题，但许多都依赖于单个指针式基础决策，将关系指令压缩为一个选择。对于细粒度查询来说，这是脆弱的，其中必须通过上下文对象和空间关系排除多个同类候选项。我们提出了结构化空间推理3D-LLM（SSR 3D-LLM），这是统一3D-LLM的结构化基础接口。给定固定的Mask 3D对象提案，LLM从查询中编写一系列潜在空间推理步骤和记忆标记，几何感知评分器读取这些潜在步骤，以便通过步进长度掩蔽逐步完善候选排名。潜在步骤是从标准基准目标监督和训练期间的辅助参考线索监督中学习的，而推理仅使用输入查询和Mask 3D提案。在ReferIt 3D、ScanReef和Multi 3DRef中，SSR 3D-LLM实现了统一3D-LLM基线中最强的结果，在细粒度基础和与之前的统一3D-LLM的一致改进方面比单指针QPG基线取得了实质性进展，同时保留了默认的语言任务路线。

2605.28023.title: VCap: Hypergeometric Rewards for Weak-to-Strong Visual Captioning
2605.28023.abstract: arXiv:2605.28023v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Visual captioning requires models to capture visual content faithfully while minimizing both omission and hallucination. As the dominant paradigm for captioning, MLLMs have achieved strong performance through scaling and high-quality data. Recently, RL has emerged as a key route to driving MLLMs toward higher precision and broader coverage, however, existing reward designs for captioning fail to provide fine-grained and reliable signals for factual verification, limiting their effectiveness. To address this, we propose VCap, a Witness-Adjudicator reward that pairs the reference caption (a witness) with the visual signal (an adjudicator). By explicitly verifying factual consistency between the reference and policy-generated captions grounded in the visual signal, VCap delivers a reward signal with hypergeometric-distribution-level precision for caption quality verification. This design enables effective learning even from imperfect references, facilitating weak-to-strong generalization in RL training. In our experiments, an 8B model trained with VCap outperforms open- and closed-source SOTA models on multiple image and video captioning benchmarks. Human evaluation further confirms its strong alignment with factual correctness. Additionally, VCap improves MLLM perceptual capability, generalizes across tasks, and surpasses best-of-N distillation, challenging prior assumptions about RLVR.
2605.28023.COMMENT: Proposes a novel hypergeometric-distribution-level reward signal for RL-based visual captioning, enabling weak-to-strong generalization.
2605.28023.title_cn: Vcap：从弱到强视觉字幕的超几何奖励
2605.28023.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.28023v1宣布类型：新摘要：视觉字幕要求模型忠实地捕捉视觉内容，同时最大限度地减少遗漏和幻觉。作为字幕的主要范式，MLLM通过扩展和高质量数据取得了强劲的性能。最近，RL已成为推动MLLM迈向更高精确度和更广泛覆盖范围的关键途径，然而，现有的字幕奖励设计未能为事实验证提供细粒度和可靠的信号，从而限制了其有效性。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了Vcap，这是一种证人-裁决者奖励，将参考说明（证人）与视觉信号（裁决者）配对。通过显式验证基于视觉信号的参考和策略生成的字幕之间的事实一致性，VCAP提供具有超几何分布水平精确度的奖励信号，用于字幕质量验证。这种设计甚至可以从不完美的引用中进行有效学习，促进RL训练中的弱到强概括。在我们的实验中，用VCAP训练的8B模型在多个图像和视频字幕基准上优于开源和闭源SOTA模型。人类评估进一步证实了其与事实正确性的高度一致性。此外，VMap还提高了MLLM感知能力，可在任务中进行推广，并超越了N中最佳蒸馏，挑战了先前关于WLVR的假设。

2605.28396.title: ADWIN: Adaptive Windows for Horizon-Aware On-Policy Distillation
2605.28396.abstract: arXiv:2605.28396v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: On-policy distillation (OPD) transfers reasoning behavior by training a student on teacher feedback along student-generated trajectories, but standard full-rollout training ties every update to a costly completion and can over-allocate supervision to late positions with low marginal value for the current student. We revisit this assumption through the useful supervision horizon: student-induced rollouts can drift from teacher-preferred continuations, while aligned prefixes may already preserve the long-horizon OPD update direction. We propose ADWIN, an adaptive-window framework for OPD that treats rollout length as an online admissibility decision, training on short teacher-anchored prefixes while using delayed full-rollout probes to audit prefix--full alignment and adapt the next horizon with staleness control. Across math and code reasoning benchmarks in single-task, multi-task, and strong-to-weak settings, ADWIN improves the accuracy--compute trade-off over full-rollout OPD and prefix-based baselines, reducing end-to-end training cost by up to 4.1 times while achieving comparable or better accuracy.
2605.28396.COMMENT: Proposes ADWIN, an adaptive-window on-policy distillation framework that improves the accuracy-compute trade-off for LLM reasoning by dynamically adjusting rollout length.
2605.28396.title_cn: ADWIN：用于地平线感知按策略蒸馏的自适应Windows
2605.28396.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.28396v1宣布类型：新摘要：按政策蒸馏（OPD）通过沿着学生生成的轨迹对学生进行教师反馈培训来转移推理行为，但标准的全面推出培训将每次更新与成本高昂的完成联系起来，并且可能会将监督过度分配给当前学生边际价值较低的后期职位。我们通过有用的监督视野重新审视这一假设：学生引发的推出可能会偏离教师偏好的延续，而对齐的前置可能已经保留了长期OPD更新方向。我们提出了ADWIN，这是OPD的一个自适应窗口框架，将推出长度视为在线可接受性决策，在教师锚定的短前置上进行训练，同时使用延迟的全面推出探测来审核前置--完全对齐并适应下一个视野与陈旧控制。在单任务、多任务和强到弱设置中的数学和代码推理基准中，ADWIN提高了准确性--与全面推出的OPD和基于后缀的基线相比，计算权衡，将端到端训练成本降低高达4.1倍，同时实现相当或更好的准确性。

2605.27760.title: SkillGrad: Optimizing Agent Skills Like Gradient Descent
2605.27760.abstract: arXiv:2605.27760v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Agent skills provide a lightweight way to adapt LLM agents to specialized domains by storing reusable procedural knowledge in structured files. However, whether downloaded from third parties or self-generated, these skills are often unreliable, incomplete, or outdated. Existing skill-evolution methods often address these deficiencies through heuristic reflections without an explicit optimization formulation. In this paper, we propose SkillGrad, a gradient-descent-inspired framework for optimizing agent skills. SkillGrad treats the skill package as a structured parameter to optimize in a gradient descent fashion: task executions provide trajectory-level loss evidence, automatic diagnoses then provide text-based gradients that indicate the correction directions. To stabilize optimization across iterations, a momentum agent accumulates recurring diagnostic patterns into a persistent memory overlay. Finally, an LLM-based patcher executes the parameter update by applying layer-aware edits to the skill package. Evaluated on SpreadsheetBench Verified and WikiTableQuestions, SkillGrad consistently outperforms training-based skill evolution baselines across two backbone LLMs, improving over the strongest training-based baseline by $6.7$ percentage points on average. Ablations further show that momentum and contrastive diagnosis both contribute to the final skill quality.
2605.27760.COMMENT: Proposes SkillGrad, a gradient-descent-inspired framework for self-improving LLM agent skills via trajectory-level loss and text-based gradients, directly addressing self-evolving agents.
2605.27760.title_cn: SkillGrad：优化像梯度下降这样的代理技能
2605.27760.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.27760v1公告类型：新摘要：代理技能提供了一种轻量级的方式，通过将可重用的过程知识存储在结构化文件中，使LLM代理适应专门的领域。然而，无论是从第三方下载还是自行生成，这些技能通常都不可靠，不完整或过时。现有的技能进化方法通常通过启发式反思来解决这些缺陷，而没有明确的优化公式。在本文中，我们提出了SkillGrad，这是一个基于梯度下降的框架，用于优化代理技能。SkillGrad将技能包视为结构化参数，以梯度下降方式进行优化：任务执行提供子级损失证据，自动诊断然后提供指示纠正方向的基于文本的梯度。为了稳定迭代期间的优化，动量代理将重复出现的诊断模式积累到持久记忆覆盖中。最后，基于LLM的修补器通过对技能包应用层感知编辑来执行参数更新。在电子表格Bench Verification和WikiTable Questions上进行评估，SkillGrad在两个骨干LLM中始终优于基于培训的技能发展基线，比最强的基于培训的基线平均提高了6.7美元个百分点。消融进一步表明，动量和对比诊断都有助于最终的技能质量。

2605.28774.title: Agent Explorative Policy Optimization for Multimodal Agentic Reasoning
2605.28774.abstract: arXiv:2605.28774v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Vision-language models with extended reasoning succeed on complex problems, but many real-world problems require external tools that internal reasoning alone often cannot resolve. Agentic reasoning therefore interleaves two behaviors with a structural asymmetry: thinking (the self-contained default) and tool use (a high-variance auxiliary acting). We refer to this asymmetry as the Thinking-Acting Gap. Under standard RL recipes like GRPO, the gap manifests as two diagnostic symptoms during training: tool use is attempted on only ~30% of rollouts, and when attempted, the tool-using rollouts within a group are all-wrong on ~40% of questions, suppressing the learning signal at the tool calls that needed it. We propose AXPO (Agent eXplorative Policy Optimization): for each all-wrong tool-using subgroup, AXPO fixes the thinking prefix and resamples the tool call and its continuation, paired with uncertainty-based prefix selection. Across nine multimodal benchmarks and three scales of Qwen3-VL-Thinking, SFT+AXPO outperforms SFT+GRPO at average (+1.8pp Pass@1 and +1.8pp Pass@4 at 8B on average) and 8B with SFT+AXPO surpasses the 32B Base on Pass@4 with 4 times fewer parameters.
2605.28774.COMMENT: Proposes AXPO, a novel RL method (RL for LLMs) that addresses the Thinking-Acting Gap in agentic reasoning by resampling tool calls with fixed thinking prefixes, improving multimodal agent performance.
2605.28774.title_cn: 多模式统计推理的Agent探索性策略优化
2605.28774.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.28774v1宣布类型：新摘要：具有扩展推理的视觉语言模型在复杂问题上取得成功，但许多现实世界的问题需要外部工具，而仅靠内部推理往往无法解决这些工具。因此，抽象推理交织了两种具有结构不对称的行为：思考（独立默认）和工具使用（高方差辅助行为）。我们将这种不对称称为思维-行为差距。在GRPO等标准RL食谱下，这种差距表现为培训期间的两种诊断症状：仅在~30%的展开中尝试使用工具，并且当尝试时，组内的工具使用展开在~40%的问题上完全错误，从而抑制了需要的工具调用中的学习信号。我们提出了AX PO（Agent eXplorative Policy Optimism）：对于每个完全错误的工具使用子组，AX PO会修复思维前置并重新采样工具调用及其延续，并与基于不确定性的前置选择相结合。在九个多模式基准测试和Qwen 3-VL-Thinking的三个量表中，SFT+ AX PO平均优于SFT+GRPO（平均8B时，+1.8pp Pass@1和+1.8pp Pass@4），8B采用SFT+ AX PO超过了Pass@4的32 B Base，参数少了4倍。

2605.28247.title: IRDS: Interpretable RLVR Data Selection via Verifier-Coupled Sparse Autoencoder Coverage
2605.28247.abstract: arXiv:2605.28247v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has become a key technique for en- hancing LLM reasoning, yet its data ineffi- ciency remains a major bottleneck. Existing methods address this problem only partially, each missing at least one of subset-level cov- erage, verifier signal use, or interpretability. To address this gap, we present IRDS (Inter- pretable RLVR Data Selection), which selects RLVR training instances on a sparse autoen- coder (SAE) cluster basis so the selection itself is auditable on recognizable problem motifs. To select instances the model both fails on and can still learn from, we introduce a verifier- coupled coverage objective on the SAE basis and solve it by greedy log-determinant max- imization. Experiments on three instruction- tuned models and six math reasoning bench- marks show that IRDS achieves the highest overall accuracy, exceeding the strongest base- line by +3.9/+4.0 pp on the two Qwen models and by +0.5 pp on Llama-3.1-8B, while run- ning an order of magnitude cheaper than the trajectory-based baseline.
2605.28247.COMMENT: Proposes a novel interpretable data selection method for RLVR that uses SAE coverage and verifier signals to improve LLM reasoning.
2605.28247.title_cn: IRDS：基于验证器耦合稀疏自动编码器覆盖的可解释RLVR数据选择
2605.28247.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.28247v1宣布类型：新摘要：带可验证奖励的强化学习（WLVR）已成为增强LLM推理的关键技术，但其数据低效仍然是一个主要瓶颈。现有方法仅部分解决了这个问题，每种方法都缺少子集级覆盖率、验证器信号使用或可解释性中的至少一个。为了解决这一差距，我们提出了IRDS（Inter-pretable WLVR Data select），它在稀疏自动编码器（SAGE）集群的基础上选择WLVR训练实例，因此选择本身是可识别的问题基序的审计的。为了选择模型既失败又仍然可以学习的实例，我们在严重不良事件的基础上引入了验证者耦合覆盖目标，并通过贪婪的日志决定性最大化来解决它。对三个指令调整模型和六个数学推理基准的实验表明，IDS实现了最高的总体准确率，在两个Qwen模型上超过了最强基线+3.9/+4.0 pp，在Lama-3.1-8B上超过了+0.5 pp，同时运行比基于航天器的基线便宜一个数量级。

2605.27858.title: DecomposeRL: Learning to Ask Useful, Informative, and Diverse Questions for Semi-Supervised, Traceable Claim Verification
2605.27858.abstract: arXiv:2605.27858v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Claim verification splits between end-to-end classifiers that are accurate but yields no inspectable traces, and decomposition-based methods produce inspectable traces but lag performance on benchmark datasets. We propose DecomposeRL an accurate claim-verifier that produce inspectable traces. DecomposeRL frames decomposition as an RL policy trained with GRPO and a multi-faceted reward ensemble, enabling both fully supervised and semi-supervised learning from unlabeled claims. DecomposeRL addresses the prohibitive training cost of GRPO with a data-curation funnel that distills 115K fact-verification claims into a compact, learning-signal-dense subset of 5K claims. We show that a DecomposeRL-7B policy trained with full supervision on only ~5K curated claims achieves 86.3 in-domain and 69.8 out-of-domain balanced accuracy across 11 claim-verification benchmarks containing biomedical, political, scientific, and general-domain claims. Despite being 4x smaller, it matches 32B baselines and GPT-4.1-mini, and it further outperforms baselines in a semi-supervised setting with only 10% labeled claims data. Code, data, and models are available at https://dipta007.github.io/DecomposeRL
2605.27858.COMMENT: Proposes DecomposeRL, a claim verification method that frames decomposition as an RL policy trained with GRPO and a multi-faceted reward ensemble, directly matching RL for LLMs via explicit reward design and RL pipeline.
2605.27858.title_cn: DecomposeRL：学习提出有用、信息丰富且多样化的问题以进行半监督、可追溯的声明验证
2605.27858.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.27858v1宣布类型：新摘要：声明验证在端到端分类器之间分裂，这些分类器准确但不会产生可检查的痕迹，而基于分解的方法会产生可检查的痕迹，但在基准数据集上的性能滞后。我们提出DecomposeRL是一种准确的声明验证器，可以产生可检查的痕迹。分解RL将分解作为使用GRPO和多方面奖励集成训练的RL策略，支持从未标记的声明进行完全监督和半监督学习。DecomposeRL通过数据策展漏斗解决了GRPO令人望而却步的培训成本，该漏斗将115 K事实验证声明提炼为紧凑的、学习信号密集的5 K声明子集。我们表明，在仅对约5000个策划声明进行全面监督的情况下训练的DecomposeRL-7 B策略在包含生物医学、政治、科学和一般领域声明的11个声明验证基准上实现了86.3的域内平衡准确性和69.8的域外平衡准确性。尽管体积小了4倍，但它与32 B基线和GPT-4.1-mini相匹配，并且在仅标记10%的索赔数据的半监督环境中进一步优于基线。代码、数据和模型可访问https://dipta007.github.io/DecomposeRL

2605.27701.title: Cross-Entropy Games and Frost Training
2605.27701.abstract: arXiv:2605.27701v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: We present Frost Training, a method for improving Monte Carlo-based policy optimization for a large family of LLM-as-a-judge tasks called Cross-Entropy Games. The key idea is to exploit the gradient of the reward function in embedding space. This signal is used in the Greedy Coordinate Gradient (GCG) jailbreaking technique; we demonstrate for the first time that it can also be used to boost model training. We validate our method using GRPO training for maximum-likelihood infilling. Frost Training improves the model's ability to generate high-scoring outputs, reaching higher maximum scores in a best-of-k setting, and does so at an increased speed.
2605.27701.COMMENT: Proposes Frost Training, a novel RL method for LLM-as-a-judge tasks that uses gradient signals from the reward function in embedding space to improve GRPO training.
2605.27701.title_cn: 交叉熵博弈与弗罗斯特训练
2605.27701.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.27701v1宣布类型：新摘要：我们介绍了Frost Training，这是一种用于改进基于蒙特卡洛的策略优化的方法，用于一大系列LLM作为法官任务（称为交叉-Entropy Games）。关键思想是利用嵌入空间中奖励函数的梯度。该信号用于贪婪坐标梯度（GCG）越狱技术;我们首次证明它也可以用于增强模型训练。我们使用GRPO训练进行最大似然填充来验证我们的方法。Frost Training提高了模型生成高分输出的能力，在k三胜设置中达到更高的最大分数，并且以更快的速度实现这一目标。

2605.27860.title: C-MIG: Multi-view Information Gain-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Clinical Diagnosis Reasoning
2605.27860.abstract: arXiv:2605.27860v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation combined with reinforcement learning has shown promise for grounding large language models in trustworthy medical evidence. However, existing methods rely on exact-match binary rewards, which in clinical diagnosis cause two issues: (i) semantically relevant but non-verbatim steps receive zero signal, discarding valuable learning signals; and (ii) uni-dimensional rewards cannot effectively supervise heterogeneous reasoning capabilities. To address these issues, we propose C-MIG, a Multi-view Information Gain-based retrieval-augmented generation framework for Clinical diagnosis. C-MIG estimates information gain under a frozen reference model from two complementary views, retrieved-document and document-refinement, to jointly guide what to retrieve and how to refine, alleviating the issues of valuable reward signal loss and credit assignment. We further design a multi-subquery retrieval augmentation strategy that improves knowledge recall coverage in clinical diagnostic scenarios. Comprehensive experiments on four medical benchmarks demonstrate that C-MIG achieves the best performance among all RAG-RL methods on both in-domain and out-of-domain sets, and outperforms state-of-the-art general-purpose LLMs for clinical diagnosis.
2605.27860.COMMENT: Proposes a novel multi-view information gain reward for RAG-RL in clinical diagnosis, addressing sparse reward and credit assignment issues.
2605.27860.title_cn: C-SYS：用于临床诊断推理的基于多视图信息收益的检索增强生成
2605.27860.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.27860v1宣布类型：新摘要：检索增强生成与强化学习相结合已经显示出将大型语言模型建立在值得信赖的医学证据中的希望。然而，现有的方法依赖于精确匹配的二元奖励，这在临床诊断中会导致两个问题：（i）语义相关但非逐字步骤接收零信号，丢弃有价值的学习信号;（ii）一维奖励无法有效地监督异类推理能力。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了C-SYS，这是一种用于临床诊断的基于多视图信息收益的检索增强生成框架。C-RST从两个互补的视图（检索文档和文档细化）估计冻结参考模型下的信息收益，以共同指导检索什么以及如何细化，缓解有价值的奖励信号丢失和信用分配问题。我们进一步设计了多子查询检索增强策略，以提高临床诊断场景中的知识召回覆盖率。对四个医学基准的综合实验表明，C-SYS在域内和域外集上都实现了所有RAG-RL方法中最好的性能，并且在临床诊断方面优于最先进的通用LLM。

2605.27846.title: EAPO: Entropy-Driven Adaptive Positive-Negative Sample Weighting for Policy Optimization in Open-Ended QA
2605.27846.abstract: arXiv:2605.27846v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large Reasoning Models are typically trained via reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (RLVR). However, existing approaches adopt fixed weights for positive and negative samples, and the conclusions hardly generalize to open-ended question answering (QA). In this paper, we systematically investigate the roles of positive and negative samples in reinforcement learning for open-ended QA. We propose a reward-mean-based strategy for distinguishing positive from negative samples, and observe that negative samples predominantly govern response diversity and the performance upper bound, whereas positive samples primarily determine response quality and convergence stability. Building on these observations, we propose EAPO, an Entropy-driven Adaptive Policy Optimization method that adaptively computes the weighting coefficients of positive samples based on the ratio of the current policy entropy to the initial entropy. During the entropy-decreasing phase, the weight assigned to positive samples is reduced to preserve exploration, whereas during the entropy-increasing phase it is amplified to reinforce stability, thereby mitigating entropy collapse. Experiments on two publicly available open-ended medical QA datasets demonstrate that EAPO consistently and substantially outperforms fixed-weight baselines in both response diversity and stability.
2605.27846.COMMENT: Proposes EAPO, an entropy-driven adaptive weighting method for positive/negative samples in RLVR for open-ended QA, directly addressing RL for LLMs with a new reward weighting scheme.
2605.27846.title_cn: EAPO：开放式QA中的政策优化的开放式QA中的开放式驱动自适应正-负样本加权
2605.27846.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.27846v1宣布类型：新摘要：大型推理模型通常通过来自可验证奖励的强化学习（WLVR）进行训练。然而，现有方法对正样本和负样本采用固定权重，结论很难推广到开放式问答（QA）。本文系统地研究了正样本和负样本在开放式问答强化学习中的作用。我们提出了一种基于奖励均值的策略来区分正样本和负样本，并观察到负样本主要决定响应多样性和性能上限，而正样本主要决定响应质量和收敛稳定性。在这些观察的基础上，我们提出了EAPO，这是一种由熵驱动的自适应政策优化方法，它根据当前政策信息量与初始信息量之比自适应地计算正样本的加权系数。在信息量递减阶段，分配给阳性样本的权重会减少以保持探索，而在信息量递减阶段，它会被放大以加强稳定性，从而减轻信息量崩塌。对两个公开的开放式医疗QA数据集的实验表明，EAPO在反应多样性和稳定性方面始终且大幅优于固定权重基线。

2605.28070.title: Bridging the Detection-to-Abstention Gap in Reasoning Models under Insufficient Information
2605.28070.abstract: arXiv:2605.28070v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: We highlight a failure mode of large reasoning models on questions with insufficient information: models may recognize that a problem is under-specified, yet still continue reasoning and produce unsupported final answers instead of abstaining. We formalize this mismatch as the detection-to-abstention gap, where detected insufficiency fails to translate into final abstention. This gap is especially concerning in high-risk domains such as medical AI, where answers based on incomplete evidence can be more harmful than refusal. To close this gap, we propose Judge-Then-Solve (JTS), a trajectory-level reasoning-control framework that trains models to make an explicit answerability commitment before solution generation. Rather than treating abstention as a final-answer style, JTS casts it as a control decision: the model either proceeds to solve or terminates early based on its answerability judgment. We instantiate this policy through supervised warm-up and missing-premise reinforcement learning with consistency and length-shaping rewards. Experiments on dense and MoE reasoning models show that JTS substantially improves reliable abstention across datasets and pushes Abstention@Detection (A@D) to near-saturation, indicating that models not only detect missing information but also act on that detection. By terminating unanswerable trajectories immediately after the answerability judgment, JTS reduces unnecessary reasoning and improves inference efficiency when continued deliberation would amplify unsupported assumptions. We also observe that missing-premise training can alter reasoning behavior on difficult but answerable problems, reducing unproductive self-reflection. These results suggest that abstention under insufficient information is a key form of reasoning control for deploying reasoning models safely and efficiently.
2605.28070.COMMENT: Introduces Judge-Then-Solve (JTS), a trajectory-level reasoning-control framework using RL with consistency and length-shaping rewards to close the detection-to-abstention gap in reasoning models.
2605.28070.title_cn: 弥合信息不足下推理模型中的检测与弃权差距
2605.28070.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.28070v1宣布类型：新摘要：我们强调了大型推理模型在信息不足的问题上的失败模式：模型可能会认识到问题未被充分指定，但仍然继续推理并产生不支持的最终答案，而不是弃权。我们将这种不匹配形式化为检测到预防的差距，其中检测到的不足未能转化为最终的预防。这种差距在医疗人工智能等高风险领域尤其令人担忧，在这些领域，基于不完整证据的答案可能比拒绝更有害。为了缩小这一差距，我们提出了“判断然后解决”（JTS），这是一个车间级推理控制框架，它训练模型在生成解决方案之前做出明确的可回答性承诺。JTS没有将回避视为最终答案风格，而是将其视为控制决策：模型要么继续解决，要么根据其可回答性判断提前终止。我们通过有监督的热身和具有一致性和长度塑造奖励的丢失前提强化学习来实例化这一政策。密集和MoE推理模型的实验表明，JTS大大提高了数据集之间的可靠描述，并将Abstentition @Detection（A@D）推向接近饱和，这表明模型不仅可以检测缺失的信息，而且还可以对该检测采取行动。通过在可回答性判断后立即终止无法回答的轨迹，JTS减少了不必要的推理并提高了推理效率，因为继续审议会放大不支持的假设。我们还观察到，缺失前提训练可以改变对困难但可以回答的问题的推理行为，减少无成效的自我反思。这些结果表明，信息不足下的回避是安全有效地部署推理模型的推理控制的关键形式。

2605.28678.title: DREAM-R: Multimodal Speculative Reasoning with RL-Based Refined Drafting, Precise Verification, and Fully Parallel Execution
2605.28678.abstract: arXiv:2605.28678v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Speculative reasoning has recently been proposed as a means to accelerate reasoning-intensive generation in large multimodal models, but its effectiveness is often constrained by misalignment between speculative drafts and target-verified reasoning. In this work, we introduce DREAM-R, a framework that substantially improves the performance of speculative reasoning. At its core, DREAM-R employs Speculative Alignment Policy Optimization (SAPO), a reinforcement-learning objective that trains draft models to generate reasoning steps that are both faithful to target trajectories and concise. We further propose a Threshold-based Verification Mechanism (TBVM) that uses a ratio-based criterion to provide stable and interpretable acceptance of speculative steps only when positive evidence clearly dominates, thereby preventing error propagation. Building on these components, we develop a Fully Parallel Speculative Reasoning (FPSR) framework that parallelizes draft generation, target-side reasoning, and verification across multi-step reasoning, enabling early stopping and clean fallback. Experiments on reasoning-heavy benchmarks demonstrate up to speedup while preserving target-model accuracy, yielding substantial efficiency gains without compromising reasoning quality.
2605.28678.COMMENT: Proposes a new RL-based objective (SAPO) for training draft models in speculative reasoning, directly matching RL for LLMs and latent reasoning criteria.
2605.28678.title_cn: DREAM-R：具有基于RL的精细起草、精确验证和完全并行执行的多模式推测推理
2605.28678.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.28678v1宣布类型：新摘要：推测推理最近被提出作为加速大型多模式模型中推理密集型生成的一种手段，但其有效性往往受到推测草案和目标验证推理之间不一致的限制。在这项工作中，我们引入了DREAM-R，这是一个可以大幅提高推测推理性能的框架。DREAM-R的核心是采用投机一致政策优化（SPP），这是一种绩效学习目标，它训练草稿模型以生成既忠实于目标轨迹又简洁的推理步骤。我们进一步提出了一种基于假设的验证机制（TBVD），该机制使用基于比率的标准，仅在肯定证据明显占主导地位时才提供对推测步骤的稳定且可解释的接受，从而防止错误传播。在这些组件的基础上，我们开发了一个完全并行推测推理（FPSR）框架，该框架将草稿生成、目标端推理和多步推理中的验证并行化，从而实现早期停止和干净的回撤。在推理密集型基准上的实验表明，可以在保持目标模型准确性的同时加快速度，在不影响推理质量的情况下获得可观的效率提升。

2605.28390.title: You Live More Than Once: Towards Hierarchical Skill Meta-Evolving
2605.28390.abstract: arXiv:2605.28390v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Test-time skill evolving is regarded as a new paradigm for enhancing deployed agentic systems. Existing works mainly focus on hard-coded skill evolving strategies or parametric learning that rely on expensive parameter updates in the underlying LLMs. In this paper, we demonstrate that test-time refinement of the skill evolving framework itself is necessary for continuous improvement of the agent systems in different downstream scenarios, and lightweight algorithmic adaptation is feasible. Specifically, we propose HiSME, a lightweight hierarchical skill meta-evolving solution that jointly optimizes skills and the skill evolving strategy by learning meta-skills from agents' task execution traces. Experiments on diverse agentic benchmarks show that meta-evolving can produce a higher-quality skill library than pure skill evolving and can derive diverse meta-skills for different scenarios, thereby facilitating future continual experience learning. Our code is temporarily public at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/HiSME-BD45.
2605.28390.COMMENT: Introduces a hierarchical skill meta-evolving framework for agents that learns meta-skills from execution traces, enabling test-time improvement of the skill evolving strategy itself.
2605.28390.title_cn: 你不止一次地生活：走向分层技能元进化
2605.28390.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.28390v1宣布类型：新摘要：测试时技能的不断发展被认为是增强已部署代理系统的新范式。现有的工作主要集中在硬编码技能进化策略或参数学习上，这些策略依赖于基础LLM中昂贵的参数更新。在本文中，我们证明了技能进化框架本身的测试时细化对于不同下游场景下的代理系统的持续改进是必要的，并且轻量级算法适应是可行的。具体来说，我们提出了HiSME，这是一种轻量级的分层技能元进化解决方案，通过从代理的任务执行轨迹中学习元技能来联合优化技能和技能进化策略。不同代理基准的实验表明，元进化可以产生比纯粹技能进化更高质量的技能库，并可以针对不同场景推导出多样化的元技能，从而促进未来的持续体验学习。我们的代码暂时在https://anonymous.4open.science/r/HiSME-BD45上公开。

2605.27736.title: Explicit Critic Guidance for Aligning Diffusion Models
2605.27736.abstract: arXiv:2605.27736v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Online reinforcement learning is becoming increasingly important for aligning diffusion models with non-differentiable objectives. However, existing methods still face limitations in assigning fine-grained credit along denoising trajectories and in realizing stable value-based optimization. We propose a state-aligned latent actor-critic framework for diffusion post-training, in which the diffusion model serves as its own timestep-conditioned value function and predicts values directly on noisy latent states. This enables trajectory-level PPO training, supports stable actor-critic optimization with simple conditioning and value pretraining strategies, and naturally allows the learned critic to be reused for inference-time steering. We further extend the framework to multi-reward optimization, where joint training with complementary rewards helps alleviate reward hacking. Across both UNet- and DiT-based backbones, our method consistently outperforms prior group-relative RL and actor-critic baselines on single-reward and multi-reward benchmarks, while test-time steering provides additional gains in generation quality.
2605.27736.COMMENT: Proposes a state-aligned latent actor-critic framework for online RL alignment of diffusion models, enabling trajectory-level PPO and multi-reward optimization.
2605.27736.title_cn: 调整扩散模型的明确批评指导
2605.27736.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.27736v1宣布类型：新摘要：在线强化学习对于将扩散模型与不可微目标保持一致变得越来越重要。然而，现有方法在沿着去噪轨迹分配细粒度信用以及实现稳定的基于值的优化方面仍然面临局限性。我们提出了一个用于扩散后训练的状态对齐的潜在行动者-批评者框架，其中扩散模型充当其自己的时步条件价值函数，并直接预测有噪音的潜在状态上的值。这实现了教师级的PPO训练，通过简单的条件反射和价值预训练策略支持稳定的演员-评论家优化，并自然允许将学习过的评论家重复用于推理时引导。我们进一步将该框架扩展到多奖励优化，其中与补充奖励的联合训练有助于减轻奖励黑客攻击。在基于UNet和DIT的主干中，我们的方法在单奖励和多奖励基准上始终优于先前的群体相对RL和演员评论家基线，而测试时引导在生成质量方面提供了额外的收益。

2605.28020.title: The Missing Piece in Pre-trained Model Evaluation: Reward-Guided Decoding Unlocks Task-Oriented Behavior Without Parameter Updates
2605.28020.abstract: arXiv:2605.28020v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: With the rapid progress of large language models (LLMs), reliably evaluating the capabilities of pre-trained LLMs has become increasingly important. The challenge is that base pre-trained models are optimized for next-token prediction and often fail to follow instructions or produce well-formed answers under standard prompting and direct decoding. As a result, benchmark performance can conflate model capability with decoding-induced failures to produce task-oriented outputs, while exposing such behavior often relies on costly post-training. Recent decodingonly approaches attempt to reshape output distributions, but such methods can be inefficient and brittle across open-ended tasks. To address these limitations, we propose Energy-Based Decoding (EBD), a training-free, reward-guided framework for activating task-oriented behaviors from frozen pre-trained LLMs across both open-ended and objective tasks. EBD augments decoding with an external lightweight reward model, steering generations toward high-utility responses while anchoring them to the pre-trained model prior through a reward-tilted target distribution. We show that EBD shifts base-model outputs toward more instructionfollowing behavior, increasing behavioral similarity to post-trained counterparts and enabling a fairer inference-time evaluation of accessible pre-trained-model behavior. Empirically, EBD outperforms baselines across five models and six benchmarks, improving Qwen3-8B-Base on AlpacaEval2.0 from 8.8 to 44.5, reducing Mistral-7B Math500 latency by 18.9x relative to prior decoding work, and remaining robust to reward-model size.
2605.28020.COMMENT: Proposes a training-free, reward-guided decoding framework (EBD) that uses a lightweight reward model to steer frozen LLMs toward task-oriented behavior, directly matching the RL for LLMs criterion via explicit reward design.
2605.28020.title_cn: 预训练模型评估中缺失的部分：奖励引导解码在无需参数更新的情况下解锁面向任务的行为
2605.28020.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.28020v1宣布类型：新摘要：随着大型语言模型（LLM）的快速发展，可靠地评估预训练LLM的能力变得越来越重要。挑战在于，基本预训练模型针对下一个令牌预测进行了优化，并且通常无法遵循指令或在标准提示和直接解码下生成形式良好的答案。因此，基准性能可能会将模型能力与解码引起的故障混为一谈，以产生面向任务的输出，而暴露这种行为通常依赖于昂贵的后期训练。最近的decodingonly方法试图重塑输出分布，但这种方法在开放式任务中效率低下且脆弱。为了解决这些限制，我们提出了基于能量的解码（EBD），这是一种无训练的奖励引导框架，用于在开放式和客观任务中激活来自冻结的预训练LLM的面向任务的行为。EBD使用外部轻量级奖励模型增强解码，将世代转向高效用响应，同时通过奖励倾斜的目标分布将它们锚定到预先训练的模型。我们表明，EBD将基础模型输出转向更多的描述跟随行为，增加了与训练后对应者的行为相似性，并能够对可访问的预训练模型行为进行更公平的推理时评估。从经验上看，EBD在五种型号和六种基准上的表现优于基线，将AlpacaEval2.0上的Qwen 3 -8B-Base从8.8提高到44.5，将Mistral-7 B Math 500延迟相对于之前的解码工作减少了18.9倍，并且对奖励型号大小保持稳健。

2605.28073.title: StoryLens: Preference-Aligned Story Rewriting via Context-Aware Narrative Enrichment
2605.28073.abstract: arXiv:2605.28073v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Story rewriting aims to adapt existing narratives to diverse reader preferences while preserving plot consistency and narrative coherence. Unlike conventional work on style transfer, we argue that effective story rewriting demands context-aware narrative enrichment beyond surface-level stylistic adaptation. Our pilot human study shows that style adaptation alone provides only marginal gains in reader satisfaction (2.3%), while context-enhanced rewriting substantially improves user preference alignment (24.5%). Motivated by this, we introduce STORYLENSBENCH, a large-scale benchmark for preference-aligned story rewriting, comprising structured story books, multi-dimensional reader preference profiles, and ranked context-aware rewritten stories. Building on this benchmark, we propose STORYLENSEVAL, a reward model for estimating reader satisfaction over rewritten stories, and STORYLENSWRITER, a two-stage rewriting model combining supervised fine-tuning with GRPO-based reinforcement learning. We further establish a comprehensive evaluation framework covering fidelity, coherence, and reader satisfaction. Experimental results demonstrate that STORYLENSWRITER consistently outperforms strong generation and personalization baselines, highlighting the importance of context-aware narrative enrichment for personalized story rewriting.
2605.28073.COMMENT: Proposes a two-stage story rewriting model combining supervised fine-tuning with GRPO-based RL, directly matching the RL for LLMs criterion with a new reward model and RL training recipe.
2605.28073.title_cn: StoryLens：通过上下文感知叙事丰富进行偏好一致的故事重写
2605.28073.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.28073v1宣布类型：新摘要：故事重写旨在使现有叙事适应不同的读者偏好，同时保持情节一致性和叙事连贯性。与传统的风格转移工作不同，我们认为有效的故事重写需要超越表面风格改编的上下文感知叙事丰富。我们的试点人类研究表明，单独风格适应只能在读者满意度方面提供边际提高（2.3%），而上下文增强重写则大大改善了用户偏好一致性（24.5%）。出于此动机，我们引入了StoryLENSBENCH，这是一个用于偏好一致故事重写的大规模基准，包括结构化故事书、多维读者偏好配置文件和排名的上下文感知重写故事。在这个基准的基础上，我们提出了STORYLENSEval，这是一种用于估计读者对重写故事满意度的奖励模型，以及STORYLENSWRITER，这是一种将监督微调与基于GRPO的强化学习相结合的两阶段重写模型。我们进一步建立了涵盖真实性、连贯性和读者满意度的全面评估框架。实验结果表明，STORYLENSWRITER始终优于强大的生成和个性化基线，凸显了上下文感知叙事丰富对于个性化故事重写的重要性。

2605.28424.title: Skill0.5: Joint Skill Internalization and Utilization for Out-of-Distribution Generalization in Agentic Reinforcement Learning
2605.28424.abstract: arXiv:2605.28424v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Equipping large language models with explicit skills has emerged as a promising paradigm for enabling autonomous agents to solve complex tasks. Agent skills can be inherently divided into general skills for broad cognitive transfer and task-specific skills for dynamic execution. However, existing skill-based reinforcement learning (RL) methods typically force a rigid choice between full externalization, which incurs prohibitive context overhead, and full internalization, which risks overfitting and knowledge conflicts. To address this dilemma, we propose Skill0.5, a novel agentic RL framework that explicitly differentiates skill treatments by combining general skill internalization with task-specific skill utilization. Driven by a dynamic, difficulty-aware router, Skill0.5 streams tasks into distinct mastery tiers to apply tailored optimization strategies: it internalizes general skills via privileged distillation to build a cognitive foundation for hard tasks, while using diagnostic probing on easy tasks to penalize shortcuts and enforce specific skill utilization. Experiments on ALFWorld and WebShop demonstrate that Skill0.5 outperforms both memory-based and skill-based RL baselines, yielding performance improvements across both in-distribution and out-of-distribution scenarios.
2605.28424.COMMENT: Proposes Skill0.5, a novel agentic RL framework that differentiates general skill internalization from task-specific skill utilization via a difficulty-aware router, improving OOD generalization.
2605.28424.title_cn: Skill 0.5：联合技能内化和利用来实现统计强化学习中的分布外概括
2605.28424.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.28424v1宣布类型：新摘要：为大型语言模型配备显式技能已成为使自治代理能够解决复杂任务的一个有前途的范式。代理技能本质上可以分为用于广泛认知转移的通用技能和用于动态执行的特定任务技能。然而，现有的基于技能的强化学习（RL）方法通常会迫使在完全外部化和完全内化之间做出严格的选择，完全外部化会导致令人望而却步的上下文负担，而完全内化则会面临过度匹配和知识冲突的风险。为了解决这一困境，我们提出了Skill 0.5，这是一个新型的代理RL框架，它通过将一般技能内化与特定任务技能利用相结合来明确区分技能治疗。在动态的困难感知路由器的驱动下，Skill 0.5将任务分为不同的掌握层，以应用量身定制的优化策略：它通过特权蒸馏内化一般技能，为艰巨任务建立认知基础，同时对简单任务使用诊断探测来惩罚捷径并强制执行特定技能的利用。ALFWorld和WebShop上的实验表明，Skill 0.5的性能优于基于内存和基于技能的RL基线，在分发内和分发外场景中都能带来性能改进。

2605.27908.title: ESC-Skills: Discovering and Self-Evolving Skills for Emotional Support Conversations
2605.27908.abstract: arXiv:2605.27908v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Existing emotional support conversation (ESC) systems mainly rely on end-to-end response generation or coarse strategy supervision, offering limited interpretability and little support for systematic skill improvement. We propose ESC-Skills, a skill-centric framework that discovers and self-evolves executable emotional support skills. We first model localized support interactions as Intervention Units (IUs), which capture state--action--outcome dynamics between seeker states, support interventions, and post-response emotional changes. Based on IUs extracted from both successful and failed ESC dialogues, we construct the ESC-Skills Bank, a repository of executable emotional support skills containing intervention guidance, applicability conditions, expected outcomes, and potential risks. To further improve robustness, we introduce a multi-profile self-evolutionary refinement framework in which an ESC agent interacts with diverse simulated seeker profiles under SAGE evaluation. The resulting interaction traces are analyzed to identify missing skills, unsafe interventions, and profile-specific failure patterns, which are then used to refine the Skills Bank through simulation-based verification. Experimental results demonstrate that ESC-Skills improves both response-level quality and dialogue-level emotional outcomes while providing more interpretable and controllable support behaviors. We will release the code, prompts, and ESC-Skills Bank at https://github.com/aliyun/qwen-dianjin.
2605.27908.COMMENT: Proposes a self-evolving skill bank for emotional support conversations, using interaction traces and multi-profile simulation to iteratively refine skills.
2605.27908.title_cn: ESC-技能：情感支持对话的发现和自我发展技能
2605.27908.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.27908v1宣布类型：新摘要：现有的情感支持对话（ESM）系统主要依赖于端到端响应生成或粗略策略监督，提供的解释性有限，对系统技能改进的支持也很少。我们提出ESC-Skills，这是一个以技能为中心的框架，可以发现和自我进化可执行的情感支持技能。我们首先将局部支持相互作用建模为干预单元（IU），它捕获寻求者状态、支持干预和反应后情绪变化之间的状态-行动-结果动态。基于从成功和失败的ECT对话中提取的IU，我们构建了ESC-Skills Bank，这是一个可执行情感支持技能的存储库，包含干预指导、适用性条件、预期结果和潜在风险。为了进一步提高鲁棒性，我们引入了一个多轮廓自进化细化框架，其中静电稳定代理在SAGE评估下与各种模拟的搜索者轮廓交互。分析产生的交互痕迹以识别缺失的技能、不安全干预措施和特定于个人资料的失败模式，然后使用这些模式通过基于模拟的验证来完善技能库。实验结果表明，ESC-Skills可以提高响应级质量和对话级情感结果，同时提供更多可解释和可控的支持行为。我们将在https://github.com/aliyun/qwen-dianjin上发布代码、提示和ESC-Skills Bank。

2605.28805.title: OmniVerifier-M1: Multimodal Meta-Verifier with Explicit Structured Recalibration
2605.28805.abstract: arXiv:2605.28805v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Visual outcomes are increasingly central to multimodal large language models, making reliable and fine-grained verification essential for scaling generalist foundation models. In this work, we investigate multimodal meta-verification, which leverages verifier-generated rationales rather than decision-only signals, and explore how to effectively incorporate meta-verification feedback into multimodal verifier training. We identify two key findings. First, symbolic verifier outputs (e.g., bounding boxes) outperform textual explanations as meta-verification rationales, enabling efficient rule-based reinforcement learning rewards while avoiding reliance on model-based rewards from auxiliary judge models. Second, decoupling reinforcement learning objectives for binary judgment and meta-verification substantially outperforms joint reward optimization, due to intrinsic differences in output structure and learning dynamics. Based on these insights, we train OmniVerifier-M1, a generalist visual verifier leveraging symbolic meta-verification and decoupled reinforcement learning. OmniVerifier-M1 provides robust verification and fine-grained error localization, and further enables M1-TTS, a verifier-driven agentic generation system achieving dynamic region-level self-correction. This approach paves the way for more reliable, interpretable, and fine-grained multimodal verification, supporting safer and more controllable foundation model deployment.
2605.28805.COMMENT: Proposes a multimodal meta-verifier using symbolic outputs and decoupled RL for fine-grained verification and self-correction, directly matching RL for LLMs via verifier-driven reward design.
2605.28805.title_cn: OmniVerifier-M1：具有显式结构化重新校准的多模式元验证器
2605.28805.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.28805v1宣布类型：新摘要：视觉结果越来越成为多模式大型语言模型的核心，因此可靠且细粒度的验证对于扩展通才基础模型至关重要。在这项工作中，我们研究了多模式元验证，它利用了验证者生成的原理而不是仅决策的信号，并探索了如何有效地将元验证反馈纳入多模式验证者训练中。我们确定了两个关键发现。首先，符号验证器输出（例如，边界框）作为元验证原理优于文本解释，实现了高效的基于规则的强化学习奖励，同时避免了对来自辅助判断模型的基于模型的奖励的依赖。其次，由于输出结构和学习动态的内在差异，将二元判断和元验证的强化学习目标脱钩大大优于联合奖励优化。基于这些见解，我们训练OmniVerifier-M1，这是一个利用符号元验证和脱钩强化学习的通才视觉验证器。OmniVerifier-M1提供稳健的验证和细粒度的错误定位，并进一步支持M1-TTC，这是一个验证器驱动的代理生成系统，可实现动态区域级自我纠正。这种方法为更可靠、可解释和细粒度的多模式验证铺平了道路，支持更安全、更可控的基础模型部署。

2605.28751.title: Extrapolative Weight Averaging Reveals Correctness-Efficiency Frontiers in Code RL
2605.28751.abstract: arXiv:2605.28751v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Linear interpolation between fine-tuned checkpoints has been shown to trace the Pareto front between competing objectives, but whether extrapolative weight averaging can extend such frontiers to new checkpoints useful at inference time, without additional RL training, remains unclear. We study this question in RL for competitive programming, where hidden unit tests under time and memory limits enforce both functional correctness and computational efficiency. Starting from a shared initialization, we train checkpoints under nested unit-test coverage: low-coverage rewards require passing smaller-input tests, while high-coverage rewards require passing progressively larger tests up to the full suite. This sweep reveals the emergence of a correctness-efficiency frontier: on hard problems, higher-coverage reward reduces optimization failures but increases correctness failures, leaving solve rate nearly unchanged. Interpolation between low- and high-coverage checkpoints recovers this frontier, while extrapolation extends it beyond the trained endpoints. Both the frontier and its extrapolative continuation appear across three inference settings, pure reasoning, tool use, and agentic coding, and across two model scales, 32B and 7B. At the problem level, moving along the frontier changes which problems are solved, making extrapolated checkpoints complementary policies in inference-time scaling. Ensembles with extrapolative weight averaging broaden coverage and improve pass@250 on LCB/hard by 3.3% over the best single checkpoint at matched sample budget. These results show that nested unit-test coverage in code RL induces a frontier that extrapolative weight averaging can navigate, extend, and exploit.
2605.28751.COMMENT: Proposes extrapolative weight averaging to navigate correctness-efficiency frontiers discovered via nested unit-test coverage rewards in code RL, enabling inference-time scaling without additional training.
2605.28751.title_cn: 推断权重平均揭示代码RL中的正确性-效率前沿
2605.28751.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.28751v1宣布类型：新摘要：微调检查点之间的线性插值已被证明可以追踪竞争目标之间的帕累托前沿，但外推权重平均是否可以将此类边界扩展到在推理时有用的新检查点，而无需额外的RL训练，目前尚不清楚。我们在竞争性编程的RL中研究这个问题，其中在时间和内存限制下的隐藏单元测试会增强功能正确性和计算效率。从共享初始化开始，我们在嵌套单元测试覆盖范围下训练检查点：低覆盖奖励需要通过较小的输入测试，而高覆盖奖励需要通过逐渐增大的测试，直到完整套件。这一横扫揭示了正确性-效率前沿的出现：在硬问题上，更高覆盖率的奖励减少了优化失败，但增加了正确性失败，使解决率几乎保持不变。低覆盖和高覆盖检查点之间的插值恢复了这一边界，而外推则将其扩展到训练的端点之外。前沿和它的外推延续出现在三个推理设置，纯推理，工具使用，和agentic编码，并在两个模型规模，32 B和7 B。在问题层面，沿着边界移动会改变解决的问题，从而使推断检查点在推理时间缩放中成为补充策略。具有外推加权平均的集合扩大了覆盖范围，并在匹配的样本预算下将LCB/hard上的通过@250提高了3.3%。这些结果表明，代码RL中的嵌套单元测试覆盖引入了外推权重平均可以导航、扩展和利用的前沿。

2605.27954.title: Cyclical Entropy Eruption: Entropy Dynamics in Agent Reinforcement Learning
2605.27954.abstract: arXiv:2605.27954v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Agentic large language models are increasingly used to solve real-world tasks by reasoning over goals, invoking tools, and interacting with external environments. Reinforcement learning provides a natural framework for improving these behaviors, and recent agent RL methods have achieved strong results across domains. However, the training dynamics of agent RL remain poorly understood, limiting our ability to diagnose instabilities and design more effective training algorithms. In this work, we identify a previously underexplored phenomenon in agent RL, which we term cyclical entropy eruption. Unlike single-turn reasoning RL, where entropy typically collapses and stays low, agent RL training exhibits unique recurring cycles of sharp entropy eruption and gradual subsidence. We decompose this dynamic into three phases and provide theoretical and empirical analyses of each, explaining the mechanisms underlying its cyclical oscillation. We further show that degenerate patterns such as sentence duplication and hallucination, once acquired during eruption, can persist and accumulate across cycles. Motivated by these findings, we propose SEAL (Separation-Enhanced Agent Learning), a lightweight auxiliary loss that separates correct and incorrect trajectories in representation space, directly targeting the root cause of entropy eruption. Experiments across multiple benchmarks, models, and RL algorithms demonstrate that SEAL stabilizes training and yields stronger downstream agent performance.
2605.27954.COMMENT: Identifies cyclical entropy eruption in agent RL training dynamics and proposes SEAL, a representation-space auxiliary loss to stabilize training and improve downstream agent performance.
2605.27954.title_cn: 周期性熵爆发：智能体强化学习中的熵动力学
2605.27954.abstract_cn: arXiv：2605.27954v1宣布类型：新摘要：抽象的大型语言模型越来越多地用于通过推理目标、调用工具和与外部环境交互来解决现实世界的任务。强化学习为改善这些行为提供了一个自然的框架，最近的代理RL方法已经在跨领域取得了强劲的结果。然而，Agent RL的训练动态仍然知之甚少，限制了我们诊断不稳定性和设计更有效训练算法的能力。在这项工作中，我们在RL剂中发现了一种以前未充分研究的现象，我们将其称为周期性熵爆发。与单轮推理RL不同，其中的信息量通常会崩溃并保持在较低水平，代理RL训练表现出独特的、急剧的信息量爆发和逐渐下降的循环。我们将这种动态分解为三个阶段，并对每个阶段进行理论和实证分析，解释其周期性振荡的机制。我们进一步表明，重复句子和幻觉等退化模式一旦在喷发期间获得，就可以在周期中持续存在和积累。受这些发现的启发，我们提出了SEAL（分离增强型代理学习），这是一种轻量级的辅助损失，可以分离表示空间中正确和不正确的轨迹，直接针对的是信息量爆发的根本原因。多个基准测试、模型和RL算法的实验表明，SEAL稳定了训练并产生更强的下游代理性能。
